name: Data Storytelling / Infographic
summary: One insight per visual, annotated charts, animated counters. Good for application rubric (50%)

color_palette.secondary: #374151 (gray-700 for chart axes and labels), #E5E7EB (gray-200 for gridlines)
color_palette.accent: #B91C1C (Vietnam red for primary data series), #D97706 (amber-gold for secondary data), #059669 (emerald for positive trends), #2563EB (blue for comparative data)
color_palette.surface: #FFFFFF (white cards for chart containers), #FEF3C7 (warm yellow for callout boxes)
color_palette.notes: Infographic palettes use 4-6 distinct colors for data series. Red and gold align with Vietnamese theme. Blue and green provide necessary contrast for multi-series charts. All colors chosen for colorblind accessibility (avoid red-green only differentiation).

typography.font_families: Be Vietnam Pro (sans-serif). Infographic style uses: light weight for axis labels, regular for body, bold for insight headlines, black for hero statistics.
typography.size_scale: Hero statistic: 72-96px (e.g., '54 dan toc'), Insight headline: 40-48px, Annotation: 28-32px, Axis label: 24-28px, Source citation: 20-24px
typography.vietnamese_diacritics_support: Good. Be Vietnam Pro at infographic sizes (28px+) handles diacritics well. Axis labels at 24px are the floor - test Vietnamese text at this size. Hero statistics with Vietnamese text (e.g., 'nguoi') render clearly at 72px+.

animation_style.approach: Data-driven animation. Numbers count up (CountUpNumber component exists). Bar charts grow from zero. Line charts draw progressively. Pie charts fill clockwise. Spring config: damping 15, stiffness 80 (smooth, not bouncy - data should feel authoritative, not playful).
animation_style.entrance_patterns: Sequential data reveal: axis appears -> gridlines fade in -> data bars/lines animate -> annotations appear -> insight headline reveals. One data point highlighted per beat.
animation_style.motion_budget: Moderate. Chart animations are purposeful (each motion reveals data). Avoid gratuitous decoration motion. CountUpNumber animations are lightweight. Bar/line chart animations use simple interpolate().

projector_readability.contrast_ratio: Very good. Infographic design prioritizes clarity. Solid white chart containers with dark text/axes. Data colors are chosen for maximum distinguishability.
projector_readability.minimum_font_sizes: Hero stats: 72px+. Chart labels: 24-28px (at the projector floor). Annotations: 28px+. All legible from back of classroom.
projector_readability.color_washout_resistance: High. Solid, saturated data colors (red, blue, gold) survive projector washout better than pastels. White chart containers with dark gridlines maintain structure even when colors shift.

wcag_contrast_score.text_on_background: Dark text #1C1917 on white #FFFFFF: 18.1:1 (AAA). Gray labels #6B7280 on white: 4.6:1 (AA). Red #B91C1C on white: 5.8:1 (AA). All data colors meet AA against white at chart element sizes.
wcag_contrast_score.recommendation: Ensure gray axis labels are at least 24px to qualify as large text for AA compliance.

projector_degradation_resistance.details: Infographic design uses thick lines (2px+ for chart elements), solid fills (not gradients), and high-contrast labels. These survive projector degradation well. The biggest risk is thin gridlines (1px) becoming invisible - use 2px minimum and moderate contrast (#D1D5DB, not #F3F4F6).

vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.quality: Good. Main text areas (insights, annotations) at 28px+ render perfectly. Axis labels at 24px are acceptable. Hero statistics with Vietnamese words at 72px+ are excellent. Chart legend text should be 24px+ for diacritic clarity.

cultural_fit.assessment: Strong for the application rubric (50% of grade). Vietnamese government reports and state media frequently use infographic-style data presentation for economic and social statistics. Data-driven presentation of national unity achievements aligns with how Vietnamese institutions communicate progress.
cultural_fit.alignment_with_topic: Strong for application section - presenting real data about ethnic groups, regional development, and national unity policies. Medium for pure theory sections which are text-heavy.

cultural_semiotics.symbols_invoked: Evidence-based reasoning, statistical authority, progress/achievement. Numbers and charts signal 'we did research' and 'we have data.' Vietnamese government communication frequently uses statistics to demonstrate socialist achievement - this format mirrors that language.
cultural_semiotics.risk: Minimal. Data presentation is culturally neutral and universally respected in academic contexts.

teacher_perception_signal.signal: Research depth and analytical thinking. Data visualization demonstrates that students went beyond the textbook to find real statistics and present them meaningfully. This directly addresses the application rubric (50%) which values connecting theory to reality. Teacher sees effort in data collection and presentation.

rubric_alignment.format_15pct: Good - clean, professional infographic design. Well-organized visual hierarchy.
rubric_alignment.theory_35pct: Medium - pure theory sections are text-heavy and don't naturally fit infographic format. Need hybrid approach with text cards for theory and data cards for application.
rubric_alignment.application_50pct: Excellent - this style is purpose-built for presenting data, statistics, and real-world evidence. Animated charts, annotated data points, and insight callouts directly serve the application rubric. This is the strongest style for the 50% application weight.

content_density_capacity.words_per_frame: 30-50 words of text plus data visualization. The 'one insight per visual' principle means each frame has a clear focal point. Annotations add context without overcrowding.
content_density_capacity.data_viz_support: Excellent - this is the style's primary strength. Bar charts, line charts, pie charts, maps, counters, comparison tables, progress indicators. BarChart and CountUpNumber components already exist.

production_effort.assessment: Medium. BarChart and CountUpNumber components exist. Need to add: line chart, pie chart, annotated callout, data card layout, and animated map overlay. Each section needs custom data research and chart design.
production_effort.migration_cost: Medium - current card-based layout can accommodate infographic panels, but theory sections need different treatment than data sections. Not a full rewrite but significant new component work.

remotion_implementation_complexity.css_needs: Moderate. Chart layouts use flexbox/grid. SVG for custom charts (lines, arcs). CSS transitions for reveals. No complex effects.
remotion_implementation_complexity.threejs_interaction: 3D background probably unnecessary - infographic style uses clean, flat backgrounds. Could remove Three.js dependency entirely for simpler renders.
remotion_implementation_complexity.render_performance: Good. SVG charts and CSS animations are lightweight. CountUpNumber uses simple number interpolation. No GPU-intensive effects.
remotion_implementation_complexity.computation_cost: Low-moderate. Chart animations are simple interpolations. SVG rendering is efficient. No backdrop-filter or blur costs.

distraction_level: Low - infographic design serves content directly. Every visual element carries data or insight. No decorative distraction.
differentiation: High. Student presentations rarely include animated data visualization. Most use static screenshots of charts at best. Animated infographic video would stand out significantly in a university class.

temporal_coherence.assessment: Good with planning. Consistent chart style, color coding, and annotation format across sections. Data cards use the same layout template. However, theory sections (35%) need a different visual treatment, requiring a hybrid approach.
temporal_coherence.risk: Theory-heavy sections (chapters 1-3 of the presentation) may feel like a different video if only infographic style is used for application sections. Need unified visual language that spans both.

dark_vs_light_mode_viability: Best on light backgrounds. Infographic/data visualization is optimized for white/light containers with dark text and colorful data elements. Light mode is the standard for professional infographics. Perfect match for projector requirements.

examples: Our World in Data - animated data visualizations for social/economic topics
Hans Rosling / Gapminder - famous animated bubble charts for development data
Vox video essays - data-driven storytelling with annotated charts
Vietnamese government infographics (Dan tri, VnExpress) - statistics on economic/social progress
Kurzgesagt - animated infographic style for educational content
precedent.academic_context: Data visualization is the gold standard for academic presentations. Infographic-style presentation is used in TED talks, academic conferences, and policy briefings. Directly relevant to social science presentation.
